description: Implement alliance bribery in Tea Ceremony - allow offering coins with alliance proposals
steps: In src/types/game.ts, extend the AllianceProposal interface to include an optional `bribeAmount?: number` field.

In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, modify `proposeAlliance(state, fromId, toId)` to accept a 4th parameter `bribeAmount: number = 0`. When creating the proposal object, include the bribeAmount. In the log, if bribeAmount > 0, mention it: 'PLAYER proposes alliance to TARGET offering X monedas'. When the alliance is auto-accepted (reverse proposal exists), also handle the bribe transfer.

In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, modify `acceptAlliance(state, fromId, toId)` to find the proposal's bribeAmount and transfer those coins from the proposer to the accepter. Log: 'ACCEPTER receives X monedas as alliance bribe from PROPOSER'.

In src/store/gameStore.ts, update `doProposeAlliance` to accept a second parameter `bribeAmount: number` and pass it to `proposeAlliance()`. Update the type definition from `(to: string) => void` to `(to: string, bribeAmount?: number) => void`.

In src/components/ActionPanel.tsx, add a coin amount input (slider or number input, 0 to current player's coins) in the Tea Ceremony alliance section. When the player clicks 'Proponer Alianza', pass the selected bribe amount to `doProposeAlliance(target, amount)`. Show the bribe amount next to pending proposals so the target can see how much is offered.
In the pending proposals display (where doAcceptAlliance is called), show the bribe amount offered alongside the clan name so the player can see the incentive before accepting.
Add needed i18n keys to both en.ts and es.ts for bribe-related text (e.g., 'actions.bribeLabel', 'actions.offeringCoins').
acceptance_criteria: Players can set a bribe amount (0 to their available coins) when proposing an alliance
When an alliance is accepted, the bribe coins transfer from proposer to accepter
The bribe amount is visible in pending proposals for the target player
Log entries mention the bribe amount when > 0
If bribeAmount is 0, behavior is identical to current (no mention of bribe)
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/shoguns-ascent && npx tsc --noEmit` and confirm zero errors
Run `cd /projects/sandbox/shoguns-ascent && npx vite build` and confirm successful build
